ddls:
CREATE TABLE "driver_standings" (
  "driver_standings_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each driver's standing in a race */,
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the race ID */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the driver ID */,
  "season_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the season ID */,
  "points" REAL /* Points scored by the driver in the race */,
  "position" INTEGER /* Final position of the driver in the race */,
  "position_text" TEXT /* Text representation of the driver's position (e.g., '1st', '2nd') */,
  "wins" INTEGER /* Number of wins by the driver in the race */,
  "lap_time" TEXT /* Fastest lap time of the driver in the race */,
  "grid_position" INTEGER /* Starting position of the driver on the grid */,
  "status" TEXT /* Final status of the driver at the end of the race (e.g., finished, retired, disqualified) */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the team ID */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("driver_standings_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "races" (
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the race */,
  "season_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the season ID */,
  "race_name" TEXT /* Name of the race (e.g., 'Australian Grand Prix') */,
  "circuit_name" TEXT /* Name of the circuit where the race is held */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the race */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the race (city and country) */,
  "distance" REAL /* Total distance of the race in kilometers */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions during the race (e.g., sunny, rainy) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "drivers" (
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the driver */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the driver */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the driver */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the driver */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the driver */,
  "career_start_year" INTEGER /* Year the driver started their F1 career */,
  "career_end_year" INTEGER /* Year the driver ended their F1 career (if applicable) */,
  "total_wins" INTEGER /* Total number of race wins in the driver's career */,
  "total_podiums" INTEGER /* Total number of podium finishes in the driver's career */,
  "total_championships" INTEGER /* Total number of world championships won by the driver */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "team_foundation_year" INTEGER /* Year the team was founded */,
  "team_owner" TEXT /* Name of the team owner */,
  "team_chief" TEXT /* Name of the team chief */,
  "total_championships" INTEGER /* Total number of world championships won by the team */,
  "total_race_wins" INTEGER /* Total number of race wins by the team */,
  "total_podiums" INTEGER /* Total number of podium finishes by the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "seasons" (
  "season_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the season */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the season */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the season */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the season */,
  "total_races" INTEGER /* Total number of races in the season */,
  "champion_driver_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the driver who won the championship in the season */,
  "champion_team_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the team that won the championship in the season */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("season_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, data analyst, team manager) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON array of permissions associated with the role (e.g., ["read", "write", "delete"]) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
